title: As deadly heat waves rise, federal policy fails to cover air conditioning
content: Federal safety net covers heating but cooling is optional. That needs to change, an LDI fellow writes.
snippet: Federal safety net covers heating but cooling is optional. That needs to change, an LDI fellow writes.

title: Towards anticipatory disaster risk financing and index insurance mechanisms for resilience building in Eastern Africa
content: As climate shocks escalate in frequency and severity across Eastern Africa, the chronic reliance on post-disaster emergency aid is proving too slow, too costly, and too inadequate to protect the region's most vulnerable communities.
snippet: As climate shocks escalate in frequency and severity across Eastern Africa, the chronic reliance on post-disaster emergency aid is proving too slow, too costly, and too inadequate to protect the regio

title: Climate change costs lives by breaking down social connection
content: Climate change is a social crisis, disrupting connection, community and belonging
snippet: Climate change is a social crisis, disrupting connection, community and belonging

title: Future big droughts may be worse than we think – NZ’s past shows why
content: For an agricultural nation like New Zealand, severe drought is one of the most ominous consequences of a warming planet.
snippet: For an agricultural nation like New Zealand, severe drought is one of the most ominous consequences of a warming planet.

title: Modeling particles reveals soil density impact on surface fault ruptures
content: The discrete element method models tens of millions of distinct particles to help understand this rare earthquake hazard and inform resilient civil engineering design.
snippet: The discrete element method models tens of millions of distinct particles to help understand this rare earthquake hazard and inform resilient civil engineering design.

title: Organization of a series of ARISE Japan Annual Symposium
content: ARISE Japan is a national network of ARISE. ARISE Japan organizes "ARISE Japan Public Symposium" as its annual flagship public symposium to discuss a focused theme to contribute to the implementation of the Sendai Framework.
snippet: ARISE Japan is a national network of ARISE. ARISE Japan organizes "ARISE Japan Public Symposium" as its annual flagship public symposium to discuss a focused theme to contribute to the implementation

title: Working through the heat: How Bengaluru’s gig workers are bearing the cost of rising temperatures
content: Gig workers are vulnerable to extreme heat because they spend most of the time outside and are exposed to severe heat conditions. This causes various health risks such as dehydration, headaches, heat stroke, heat rashes and exacerbate co-morbidities.
snippet: Gig workers are vulnerable to extreme heat because they spend most of the time outside and are exposed to severe heat conditions. This causes various health risks such as dehydration, headaches, heat

title: Heatwaves are now everyday disasters – governments need to do more to protect people
content: Alongside early warning systems, public messaging and longer-term measures such as urban greening to reduce temperatures, governments need to do far more to help people stay cool when extreme temperatures hit.
snippet: Alongside early warning systems, public messaging and longer-term measures such as urban greening to reduce temperatures, governments need to do far more to help people stay cool when extreme temperat

title: Atlantic island narrowly escaped ‘stealthy’ eruption
content: Thousands of earthquakes affecting Portugal’s São Jorge Island in the Azores in March 2022 were triggered by a vast sheet of magma (molten rock) rising from more than 20km below Earth’s surface and stalling just 1.6km beneath the island.
snippet: Thousands of earthquakes affecting Portugal’s São Jorge Island in the Azores in March 2022 were triggered by a vast sheet of magma (molten rock) rising from more than 20km below Earth’s surface and st

title: Summers are getting longer each year, and it isn’t all fun and games
content: Do you have the sense that summers feel different than when you were younger? That they start earlier, arrive quickly and remain intense until the fall?
snippet: Do you have the sense that summers feel different than when you were younger? That they start earlier, arrive quickly and remain intense until the fall?

title: The solution to desert wildfire recovery could come from your salad bowl
content: ASU researchers turn to fungi as a cost-effective, sustainable way to stabilize land after a fire.
snippet: ASU researchers turn to fungi as a cost-effective, sustainable way to stabilize land after a fire.

title: New directions in mapping ice sheet fabrics and flow
content: Polarimetric radar advances enable scientists to measure orientations of crystals, bubbles, and other properties that affect the flow of glaciers and ice sheets—and their contributions to sea level.
snippet: Polarimetric radar advances enable scientists to measure orientations of crystals, bubbles, and other properties that affect the flow of glaciers and ice sheets—and their contributions to sea level.

title: Disconnected defenses: extreme weather risk across corporates, cities and financial systems
content: Extreme weather is already shaping financial outcomes across the real economy.
snippet: Extreme weather is already shaping financial outcomes across the real economy.

title: Usable knowledge for decision-making in climate adaptation- Professor Maria Carmen Lemos (University of Michigan, USA)
content: This conference addresses one of the most pressing contemporary challenges in disaster risk reduction: the production and use of actionable scientific knowledge to support decision-making in the context of climate adaptation.
snippet: This conference addresses one of the most pressing contemporary challenges in disaster risk reduction: the production and use of actionable scientific knowledge to support decision-making in the conte

title: Danger season is here again, with triple the danger for 2026
content: For the past five years, the Union of Concerned Scientists has been tracking the climate extremes of what we call “Danger Season”—the period between May and October when North America is hit hardest by extreme weather.
snippet: For the past five years, the Union of Concerned Scientists has been tracking the climate extremes of what we call “Danger Season”—the period between May and October when North America is hit hardest b

title: Assessing the resilience of global grain supplies to compound climatic and non-climatic shocks
content: Using a new global bilateral trade model representing 177 countries and four major staple crops (maize, wheat, rice, soybean), the authors simulate the food supply, trade and price impacts resulting from climate-related yield variability, and shocks

title: Property flood resilience still held back by evidence gaps, government-backed research warns
content: Property flood resilience (PFR) measures could play a far greater role in helping communities adapt to rising flood risk across the UK, but major gaps in evidence, public understanding and long-term performance data continue to limit uptake.
snippet: Property flood resilience (PFR) measures could play a far greater role in helping communities adapt to rising flood risk across the UK, but major gaps in evidence, public understanding and long-term p

title: What is El Niño? And why does it matter in a warming world?
content: El Niño is a natural climate pattern influencing global weather. Learn why it is increasingly important to understand in a warming world.
snippet: El Niño is a natural climate pattern influencing global weather. Learn why it is increasingly important to understand in a warming world.

title: Humid heat: a growing health risk in a warming climate
content: The combination of extreme heat and humidity is dangerous and becoming more common in our warming climate.
snippet: The combination of extreme heat and humidity is dangerous and becoming more common in our warming climate.

title: Inoperability assessment of interdependent critical infrastructures exposed to natural hazards considering climate change
content: Critical Infrastructures (CIs) are interdependent and, thus, vulnerable to scenarios of cascading effects initiated by natural hazardous events such as flooding, windstorms, and heat waves, which can lead to partial or complete inoperability.
snippet: Critical Infrastructures (CIs) are interdependent and, thus, vulnerable to scenarios of cascading effects initiated by natural hazardous events such as flooding, windstorms, and heat waves, which can

title: Climate, the environment and site management – overview
content: Site Management translates climate and environmental commitments into concrete, site-level action across multiple contexts.
snippet: Site Management translates climate and environmental commitments into concrete, site-level action across multiple contexts.

title: Fire that scorched African mountain range was unprecedented in the last 12,000 years, research shows
content: A study of ancient lake sediments reveals that a recent wildfire high in the Rwenzori Mountains was the first in 12 millennia, signaling a novel threat to Africa’s unique alpine ecosystems.
snippet: A study of ancient lake sediments reveals that a recent wildfire high in the Rwenzori Mountains was the first in 12 millennia, signaling a novel threat to Africa’s unique alpine ecosystems.

title: The long shadow of Maui’s wildfires
content: A therapist says many of his patients are still struggling with mental health consequences, three years after the disaster.
snippet: A therapist says many of his patients are still struggling with mental health consequences, three years after the disaster.

title: Lessons Management Forum 2026
content: The 2026 Lessons Management Forum will be a hybrid event held at Brisbane Marriott Hotel, Queensland and online.
snippet: The 2026 Lessons Management Forum will be a hybrid event held at Brisbane Marriott Hotel, Queensland and online.

title: How AI Is changing climate science
content: Artificial intelligence is beginning to transform climate science, not just by improving forecasts, but by helping researchers understand the physical forces shaping the planet’s future.
snippet: Artificial intelligence is beginning to transform climate science, not just by improving forecasts, but by helping researchers understand the physical forces shaping the planet’s future.

title: Study provides rule of thumb to estimate land sustainability in river deltas
content: A study found that a well-established relationship called Hack’s law can be used to simplify the design and construction of structures used to manage water flow and reduce flood risks along river deltas like the Po River Delta in Italy.
snippet: A study found that a well-established relationship called Hack’s law can be used to simplify the design and construction of structures used to manage water flow and reduce flood risks along river delt

title: Indonesia may soon lose its last glaciers
content: Scientists estimate that Indonesia will lose its two remaining glaciers by 2030—a warning for glaciers around the world.
snippet: Scientists estimate that Indonesia will lose its two remaining glaciers by 2030—a warning for glaciers around the world.

title: How much do we know about the mental health economic costs of disasters?
content: This literature review focuses on the economic quantifications of the mental health consequences of disasters and how these mental health impacts can shape people’s economic trajectories.
snippet: This literature review focuses on the economic quantifications of the mental health consequences of disasters and how these mental health impacts can shape people’s economic trajectories.

title: Under strain- international insights into electricity grid outages
content: As climate change accelerates, the resilience of electricity infrastructure is emerging as a critical challenge for countries worldwide.
snippet: As climate change accelerates, the resilience of electricity infrastructure is emerging as a critical challenge for countries worldwide.

title: Assessment of land subsidence and future flood hazards for coastal cities of the Gulf of Mexico using time series InSAR
content: As sea levels rise and urban land continues to sink, coastal cities along the Gulf of Mexico face a compounding flood threat that demands urgent, data-driven action.
snippet: As sea levels rise and urban land continues to sink, coastal cities along the Gulf of Mexico face a compounding flood threat that demands urgent, data-driven action.

title: From Belém to Bonn: Operationalizing the global goal on adaptation indicators
content: With SB64 convening in Bonn, Parties are preparing to take the next steps from COP30. This webinar will unpack what was agreed at COP30 related to the GGA and what operationalizing the indicators will require technically and politically.
snippet: With SB64 convening in Bonn, Parties are preparing to take the next steps from COP30. This webinar will unpack what was agreed at COP30 related to the GGA and what operationalizing the indicators will

title: Lao PDR climate change institutional assessment (CCIA)
content: As climate change intensifies across Southeast Asia, the institutional capacity of governments to translate climate commitments into coordinated policy and funded action has never been more critical.
snippet: As climate change intensifies across Southeast Asia, the institutional capacity of governments to translate climate commitments into coordinated policy and funded action has never been more critical.

title: Landslides are New Zealand’s most expensive natural hazard, and the costs are rising quickly
content: New evidence from the Natural Hazards Commission – Toka Tū Ake (NHC) shows that landslides are now New Zealand’s most costly natural hazard.
snippet: New evidence from the Natural Hazards Commission – Toka Tū Ake (NHC) shows that landslides are now New Zealand’s most costly natural hazard.

title: The economic implications of earthquake parameters for formulating financial risk management strategies: the Dead Sea Fault, Israel
content: In this study, a systematic and comprehensive methodology is developed to identify the key parameters and facilitate the design of an appropriate parametric mechanism, potentially in the form of Cat Bonds.
snippet: In this study, a systematic and comprehensive methodology is developed to identify the key parameters and facilitate the design of an appropriate parametric mechanism, potentially in the form of Cat B

title: Building resilience in Europe and Central Asia: A smart investment for growth and jobs
content: The data is clear: Investments in adaptation and resilience deliver high economic returns but remain insufficient and poorly targeted.
snippet: The data is clear: Investments in adaptation and resilience deliver high economic returns but remain insufficient and poorly targeted.

title: After disasters: stories from Talisay
content: Filipinos’ ideas about how to improve disaster response and recovery. Distributed to local communities, local government partners and high schools to support teaching on DRR.
snippet: Filipinos’ ideas about how to improve disaster response and recovery. Distributed to local communities, local government partners and high schools to support teaching on DRR.

title: Hantavirus: A cruise ship, a deer mouse and the fictional line between human and animal health
content: In February 2025, the classical pianist Betsy Arakawa died. Her husband, the actor Gene Hackman, died a week later of heart disease. The pathogen that killed her was hantavirus, almost certainly picked up from deer mouse droppings on the property.

title: Climate-induced internal migration for domestic work in India: Gendered risks and policy responses
content: This issue brief examines how climate change is reshaping internal migration patterns in India, particularly among women and adolescent girls entering informal domestic work in urban centres.
snippet: This issue brief examines how climate change is reshaping internal migration patterns in India, particularly among women and adolescent girls entering informal domestic work in urban centres.

title: Assessing pluvial flooding risk in urban areas with high spatial heterogeneity using a fused physically-based and data-driven framework
content: This study developed an integrated urban flood risk assessment framework for cities with significant spatial heterogeneity, combining differentiated hydrological-hydrodynamic modeling with complementary grid-based.

title: Integrated method for fragility assessment of railway embankments under rainfall-induced landslides
content: This work presents a climate change-informed modeling framework for assessing the inoperability of interdependent critical infrastructures exposed to NHs.
snippet: This work presents a climate change-informed modeling framework for assessing the inoperability of interdependent critical infrastructures exposed to NHs.
